'''Creature of Fenkenstrain''' is a quest where you become the assistant of [[Dr Fenkenstrain]] and slowly uncover the history of his castle: the [[Lordship of the North Coast]].

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Read the [[signpost (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|signpost]] in the middle of [[Canifis]].
|startmap = 3488,3485
|difficulty = Intermediate
|description = [[Dr Fenkenstrain]], master and sole occupant of the castle to the north-east of [[Canifis]], needs a new servant to go on a dark errand for him.

Do you have the stomach to help Fenkenstrain complete his twisted purpose?
|length = Medium
|requirements = * Completion of [[Priest in Peril]]
* Partial completion of [[The Restless Ghost]]

* {{SCP|Thieving|25|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} {{Questreqstart|no}}
* Able to defeat a level 51 monster and run past level 72 monsters

* 3 [[bronze wire]] (unnoted)
* [[Needle]] ([[costume needle]] does not work)

|recommended = * {{SCP|Combat|30|link=yes}}

==Walkthrough==
{{Map|3549,3551|align=right|mtype=square|width=200|height=200|zoom=1|caption=The start point.}}

:'''Note:''' If you are only interested in unlocking access to the [[Experiment Cave]], you do not need any of the required items - follow the Gravedigging section to obtain the star amulet, then skip digging at the grave and go straight to the memorial to enter the cave.

===Job interview===
[[File:Dr Fenkenstrain chathead.png|left|frameless]]
To start the quest, read the [[signpost (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|signpost]] located in [[Canifis]] - it is advertising a job vacancy for a 'Braindead Butler'. If the signpost says you do not meet the requirements, speak to Dr Fenkenstrain directly and he will still offer you the job, allowing you to start the quest.

Pick up the [[pickled brain]] from the table in the pub to the south, and [[Roavar]] the innkeeper will offer to sell it to you for 50 coins. Alternatively, use [[Telekinetic Grab]] to avoid paying.

Follow the path to the north east to [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]], and talk to [[Dr Fenkenstrain]] located just inside. As part of the job interview, he will ask you to describe yourself in one word; answer "braindead", then tell him that your best skill is "grave-digging". He will ask you to find some "dead stuff" - he is planning to stitch together a body and bring it to life. 

The body parts you need to find are the [[pickled brain]] you already picked up, a [[decapitated head]], [[Torso (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|torso]], [[arms]] and [[legs]]; you can retrieve these in any order.

===Gravedigging===
{{Needed|A [[spade]]|recommended=[[Armour]], [[food]], and a [[holy symbol]] is recommended for protection against [[Feral Vampyre]]s and [[leech]]es.}}
{{Map|3608,3491|align=right|mtype=square|width=200|height=200|r=2|caption=[[Gardener Ghost]]'s grave location.}}
[[File:Star amulet detail.png|left|70px|frameless]]
Upstairs in [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]], there are bookcases in both the west and the east rooms. Search the west bookcase for "The Joy of Grave Digging" and you will find a [[marble amulet]]. Search the east bookcase for "Handy Maggot Avoidance Techniques" and you will find an [[obsidian amulet]]. Use them on each other to get a [[star amulet]].

Head to the [[grave (Haunted Woods)|grave]] located slightly south-east of [[fairy ring]] {{Fairycode|ALQ}} (see map). Using "Read" on the grave will confirm the grave as Ed Lestwit's. Right click to dig the grave, and you will receive a [[decapitated head]]. '''Use the [[pickled brain]] on it'''.
{{Map|3578,3527|align=right|mtype=square|width=200|height=200|r=4|caption=The location of the memorial.}}

Just east of [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]] and west of the Farming patches, there are some tombstones. Use the [[star amulet]] on the furthest [[memorial (Experiment cave)|memorial]] to the east with the indent on it and push it to enter a dungeon that contains unaggressive level 25 and 51 [[Experiment]]s. 

[[File:Experiment safespot.png|thumb|left|200px|Experiment safespot.]]
Kill a level 51 wolf-woman [[Experiment]] to get a [[cavern key]]. An experiment in a crevice to the south west may be [[safespotted]] with ranged or magic, or [[flinched]] with melee by attacking from the crevice entrance.

:'''Note''': If you have started [[Rag and Bone Man II]], you may wish to kill the level 25 experiments here until you receive an [[Experiment bone]].

Once you have the cavern key go through the north-west passage until you reach a gate. Go up the ladder, then dig at the three [[grave (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|grave]]s to the north and you get [[legs]], [[arms]], and a [[torso]].
{{Clear|left}}

===Lightning===
{{Needed|[[Ghostspeak amulet]], [[decapitated head]], [[pickled brain]], [[arms]], [[legs]], [[torso]], [[needle]], 5 [[thread]], 3 [[bronze wire]], and a [[silver bar]].|skills={{SCP|Crafting|20}} {{Boostable|yes}} to create an [[extended brush]]}}
[[File:Creature of Fenkenstrain - The Body.png|thumb|200px|The body after all that trouble.]]
[[File:Creature of Fenkenstrain - repairing conductor.gif|thumb|left|160px|The conductor is repaired.]]
Push the memorial you came out of, and retrace your steps through the cavern. Exit the cavern and follow the path west until you reach [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]].

If you haven't already, use the [[pickled brain]] on the [[decapitated head]], and give the body parts to [[Fenkenstrain]]. He will ask you for a [[needle]] and five spools of [[thread]] to sew the body together. Give them to him, and he will ask you to repair the lightning conductor, so that he may harness the power of lightning to bring the creature to life.
[[File:Creature of Fenkenstrain - Cleaning the Fireplace.png|thumb|200px|Cleaning the Fireplace.]]

Wearing your ghostspeak amulet, ask the [[Gardener Ghost]] in the back of the castle for the [[shed key]], then go inside the shed next to him and search the [[Cupboard (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|cupboard]] for the [[garden brush]].

Take three of the [[garden cane]]s just east of the shed and use them on the brush with the [[bronze wire]] in your inventory ({{SCP|Crafting|20}}). This will create an [[extended brush]], making it suitable for chimney sweeping. Go back inside, take the staircase to the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} and use it on the fireplace in the west room to get a [[conductor mould]]. Make sure you use it on the [[Fireplace (Fenkenstrain's Castle)|centre of the fireplace]] and not the [[fireplace surround]].

Once you have the mould, you need to go to any [[furnace]] with a [[silver bar]] in your inventory to make a [[conductor]] (via the 'make lightning rod' option). The nearest furnace is in [[Port Phasmatys]] (2 ecto-tokens needed to enter, free if [[Ghosts Ahoy]] has been completed). Alternatively, use a teleport to a furnace (such as [[Amulet of glory]] teleport to [[Edgeville]]) if you have a method to easily return to the castle (such as [[fairy ring]] {{fairycode|ALQ}}).

'''Note:''' On some occasions, you cannot make the conductor. Re-logging may fix this issue; in some cases, you have to talk to the gardener to get the option to make the conductor in the furnace.

Go back to [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]]; take the stairs on either the eastern or western side of the castle up to the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}}, walk to the room furthest south, and climb the ladder to the roof. Click on the [[lightning conductor]] to fix it. Lightning will instantly strike, and the monster comes to life!

===Lord Rologarth===
{{Needed|skills={{SCP|Thieving|25}} {{Boostable|yes}} to pickpocket Dr Fenkenstrain}}
[[File:Creature of Fenkenstrain - He Lives!.png|thumb|200px|It's alive! ...and it talks!]]
[[File:Lord Rologarth chathead.png|left|frameless]]

Talk to [[Dr Fenkenstrain]] again. He will tell you that the monster tried to kill him, so he locked it in the tower. He will give you the [[tower key]] and tell you to kill the monster. Go upstairs again, and enter the central room of the upstairs section, climb the ladder, and talk to Fenkenstrain's monster.

The monster will introduce himself as [[Lord Rologarth]]. He will tell you the true story of how Fenkenstrain tricked him into selling all of his subjects and himself to the [[vampyre]]s, and ask you to stop him from carrying out any more experiments.

You need to steal from Fenkenstrain a powerful ring which is allowing him to raise the dead. Go back downstairs and pickpocket him to obtain the [[ring of charos]] ({{SCP|Thieving|25}}).

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Creature of Fenkenstrain
|image = [[File:Creature of Fenkenstrain reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 2
|rewards = 
*{{SCP|Thieving|1,000}} [[Thieving]] experience
*The [[Ring of charos]], which allows access to the [[Werewolf Agility Course]], and, when activated during [[Garden of Tranquillity]], reduced cost to many transport links.
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of Creature of Fenkenstrain is required for the following:
*[[Garden of Tranquillity]]
*[[The Great Brain Robbery]]
*[[Rag and Bone Man II]] (Only partial completion of Creature of Fenkenstrain required)

==Transcript==
{{hastranscript|Quest}}

==Trivia==
*Although not necessary for the completion of this quest, you can find a [[Letter (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|letter]] when winding the clock upstairs in [[Fenkenstrain's Castle]]. This letter, written by [[Lord Rologarth]] (the true owner of the castle), reveals the dark story of [[Dr Fenkenstrain]]'s past.
*The grave examine reveals a bit of funny wordplay regarding the unlucky gardener's name, "Here lies Ed Lestwit". Roughly pronounced Headless Twit.
*The quest is based heavily on the concept of the story of ''{{wp|Frankenstein}}'' by {{wp|Mary Shelley}}.
*Dr Fenkenstrain's examine text ''Dr Fenkenstrain, I presume'' is likely a reference to the famous quote ''Dr Livingstone I presume''.
*After the quest Dr Fenkenstrain's note on the signpost in Canifis is replaced by a new note just saying "AAARRGGGHHHHH!!!!!"
*Based on the body parts that were exhumed, you can calculate the average weight of an NPC to be around 35kg.
*The book "The Joy of Grave Digging" is a reference to Irma S. Rombauer's famous cooking book "The Joy of Cooking".

{{Creature of Fenkenstrain}}
